concerns.text: The plan proposes using 'Synthetic Proxy Datasets' (random text/math) to evaluate 'reasoning' (GSM8K/ARC) and 'calibration' (ECE). This is a category error. Randomly generated data has no ground-truth logic or semantic distribution to measure reasoning or calibration against. Evaluating a model on random noise will yield metrics that are mathematically determined by the noise generation process, not the model's intelligence. This renders the 'validation' of the self-improvement loop tautological and scientifically void.

The plan claims to track 'performance trajectories' on GSM8K/ARC. If the fallback to synthetic data is triggered (as noted in 'Dataset Strategy'), the resulting 'accuracy' metrics are meaningless. A model cannot 'improve' at solving random math problems because the problems have no solution. The analysis cannot return a negative/null result; it will only return noise, making the claimed 'empirical' relationship non-existent.

The plan applies 'paired bootstrap testing' to compare baseline vs. post-modification metrics on synthetic data. Since the synthetic data lacks semantic structure, any observed 'improvement' is a statistical artifact of random variance in the noise generation, not a causal result of architectural modification. The statistical method is valid, but the underlying data generation invalidates the causal inference entirely.

The assumption that 'OpenWebText subset... provides adequate training signal' is contradicted by the fallback plan. If the fallback to synthetic data is used, the training signal is random noise. Training a model on random noise and claiming 'recursive self-improvement' based on performance on random noise is a fundamental scientific flaw. The experiment design does not distinguish between 'learning' and 'overfitting to noise'.
